title: HTTP Secure (HTTPS)
description: HTTP over TLS/SSL - encrypted web communication.
fullExplanation: HTTPS is HTTP wrapped in TLS/SSL encryption. When you visit https://example.com, your browser establishes a TLS connection first (handshake, certificate verification), then sends HTTP requests through the encrypted tunnel. This protects against eavesdropping and tampering. The padlock icon in browsers indicates HTTPS. Modern web requires HTTPS — browsers mark HTTP sites as 'Not Secure'.

howItWorks.description: Client connects to server on port 443
Negotiate cipher suite, verify certificate, exchange keys
Send HTTP requests/responses through encrypted tunnel
TLS session can be resumed for faster subsequent connections

keyFacts: Green padlock icon indicates valid HTTPS
HSTS forces browsers to always use HTTPS
Let's Encrypt provides free SSL certificates
TLS 1.3 is faster than TLS 1.2 (fewer round trips)

components.name: SSL/TLS Certificate
components.description: Digital certificate that proves server identity, contains public key

components.name: Certificate Authority (CA)
components.description: Trusted organization that issues certificates (DigiCert, Let's Encrypt)

components.name: Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)
components.description: System of CAs, certificates, and trust chains

components.name: Cipher Suite
components.description: Set of algorithms for key exchange, encryption, and authentication
